CSCE 727 Syllabus

Section 600 (58447)

Algorithmic Foundations of Big Data Computing

Spring 2026 - College Station

End Date: 05/05/2026

Meeting Location:  ZACH 592

Instructor Details

Victoria Crawford

Preferred Contact Method

Catalog Description

Algorithmic Foundations of Big Data Computing. (3-0). Credit 3. Design and analysis

of algorithms to analyze large sets of data in fields such as machine learning and

data mining; streaming algorithms; parallel algorithms; sublinear time algorithms;

sketching algorithms; applications of these algorithms; theoretical analysis of these

algorithms as well as practical implementations for real problems. Prerequisites:

CSCE 629 or approval of instructor.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  CSCE 629 or approval of instructor.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Describe the algorithmic challenges associated with datasets that are too large to fit

into the memory of a single computer.

Describe and compare a variety of approaches that previously have been developed

in the research literature for this challenging setting.

Develop and analyze their own algorithms for a variety of applications.

Special Course Designation

None

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

Describe the algorithmic challenges associated with datasets that are too large to fit

into the memory of a single computer.

Describe and compare a variety of approaches that previously have been developed

in the research literature for this challenging setting.

Develop and analyze their own algorithms for a variety of applications.

Special Course Designation

None

Textbook and/or Resource Materials

This material Is: Optional

No textbook needed

Grading Policy

Exams  – Students’ understanding of the material will be assessed using a sequence of

three proctored exams (see schedule below).

Graded Attendance –  Class participation will be expected. Attendance will be taken at

each class. The grade for attendance will be the number of attended lectures, or excused

absences divided by the total number of classes. If a student has an excused absence, they

should provide documentation within one week after the missed class.

Project - Each student will do a project on a topic of their interest related to the course. The

project grade will be based on the implementation, and a presentation made to the class

presenting the project.

Grading policy:

Attendance: 10%

3 exams covering material from class: 60% (20% each)

Project: 20%

Project presentation: 10%

Grade range:

90-100% A

80-90% B

70-80% C

60-70% D

Below 60% F

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).
